output.final_deliverable:
Answer:
1) The capital city of Australia is Canberra.

Evidence (authoritative source):
2) According to the Encyclopaedia Britannica page for Canberra (https://www.britannica.com/place/Canberra):
"Canberra, federal capital of the Commonwealth of Australia." https://www.britannica.com/place/Canberra

Source cited:
- https://www.britannica.com/place/Canberra
output.action_summary: The capital of Australia is Canberra, as stated by Encyclopaedia Britannica: "Canberra, federal capital of the Commonwealth of Australia." (https://www.britannica.com/place/Canberra)

results.title: Canberra | History, Map, Population, Climate, & Facts | Britannica
results.description: Canberra, federal capital of the Commonwealth of Australia. It occupies part of the Australian Capital Territory, in southern Australia, and is about 150 miles (240 km) southwest of Sydney.

results.title: Australia | History, Cities, Population, Capital, Map, & Facts | Britannica
results.description: Australia, the smallest continent and one of the largest countries on Earth, lying between the Pacific and Indian oceans in the Southern Hemisphere. Australia’s capital is Canberra, located in the southeast between the larger and more important economic and cultural centres of Sydney and Melbourne.

results.description: Australian Parliament House; Museum ... Democracy at Old Parliament House (bottom), Canberra, Austl.(more) ... Australian Capital Territory (A.C.T.), political entity of the Commonwealth of Australia consisting of Canberra, the ...

results.description: The government held a competition to choose the design for the new city. The American architect Walter Burley Griffin created the winning design. Canberra officially became the capital in 1927.

results.description: The Australian Capital Territory is a region in Australia that includes the country’s capital city of Canberra. It is surrounded by the state of New South Wales. Geography…

Britannica Kids notes that "Canberra officially became the capital in 1927": https://kids.britannica.com/kids/article/Canberra/345881 (search node: 95ddd4ae-7d0b-41a9-9907-1010ebb4430f).

The Australian Capital Territory (A.C.T.) is documented by Britannica as the political entity that contains Canberra: https://www.britannica.com/place/Australian-Capital-Territory (search node: 95ddd4ae-7d0b-41a9-9907-1010ebb4430f).
